---
title: "Exercise 4C: Options Data Dashboard"
subtitle: "MGMT 675: Generative AI for Finance"
format:
  pdf:
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{xcolor}
        \PassOptionsToPackage{colorlinks=true,urlcolor=blue,linkcolor=blue}{hyperref}
---

Build a FastAPI dashboard for exploring options market data.

\bigskip
\noindent\textbf{Background.}
Download \href{https://mgmt675.kerryback.com/files/get_options.py}{get\_options.py}, which pulls 15-minute delayed options data from the CBOE (Chicago Board Options Exchange) via Yahoo Finance. It exposes a single function:

\begin{verbatim}
from get_options import get_options_data

data = get_options_data("AAPL", weeks_out=4, option_type="call")
# Returns a dict with keys:
#   "chain"       — DataFrame with Strike, Bid, Ask, Mid, ...
#   "expiration"  — selected expiration date
#   "stock_price" — last traded stock price
\end{verbatim}

Place \texttt{get\_options.py} in your project folder and use it as the data layer for your app.

\bigskip
\noindent\textbf{Requirements.}
Ask Claude Code to build a FastAPI app with the following features:

\begin{enumerate}
  \item \textbf{Inputs:} ticker symbol, weeks out (integer), and option type (call or put).
  \item \textbf{Update button:} clicking it fetches fresh data from Yahoo Finance using \texttt{get\_options\_data}.
  \item \textbf{Plot:} display bid--ask midpoints (the \texttt{Mid} column) against strike prices.  Mark the current stock price on the plot.
  \item \textbf{Data table:} display the full options chain below the plot.
\end{enumerate}

The app should ensure all required libraries are installed before trying to import them.

\bigskip
\noindent\textbf{Deliverables.}

\begin{itemize}
  \item Code (\texttt{4C-Code.zip}) --- your FastAPI app
  \item Screenshot of the running app in your browser showing a plot and data table (\texttt{4C-Screenshot.png})
\end{itemize}
